title: Marbella Boat Rental Guide: Costs, Seasons & Tips for 2026
html:
Planning a boat hire on the Costa del Sol from Marbella? With departure points at Puerto Banús and Marina Marbella, you can expect to see prices from around €300 for a half-day motorboat rental. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own, connecting you with verified skippers and yacht owners.

Where to set off in Marbella

The main sailing hubs are Puerto Banús and the smaller Marina Marbella, both offering fuel docks and chandlers. Cabopino Marina, a short drive east, is a tranquil alternative with direct access to the Cabopino dunes. For longer excursions, you can head past the bay of Marbella towards the cliffs of Marbella’s eastern coast or cross to Calahonda. You can boat hire Marbella for your chosen dates.

West along the coast, you can visit Estepona’s marina or stop at the secluded beaches near Sotogrande. The Strait of Gibraltar is about 40 nautical miles south, but most boat rentals stick to the shelter of the Costa del Sol.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motorboat, ideal for a fast run to Elviria or a sunset cruise, can take up to 8 guests from €350 half-day.
  2. Sailing yacht, perfect for a relaxed day sail towards Fuengirola, accommodates 6-10 guests from €500 half-day.
  3. Catamaran, spacious for groups up to 12, stable for anchoring in the bay of Marbella, from €700 half-day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht, such as the Fairline Targa 43 or a Maiora 26m, for up to 12 guests with a crew, from €1,500 half-day.

If you prefer a specific model, you can check the Fairline Targa 43 listing on BoatHire24 for details and availability.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella?

Prices vary from €100 per person for a shared tour up to €3,000 per day for a crewed 50-foot yacht. You can break down typical costs below.

  • Shared tour on a catamaran: from €50 per person (3 hours).
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): €300, €500.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): €1,000, €2,000.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day: €2,000, €5,000.

Currency for all quotes is EUR.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella?

No, you do not need a licence if you charter a boat with a skipper, and bareboat rentals in Spain require a valid licence equivalent to the Spanish PER. For bareboat charters, the operator will specify the exact licence needed; always confirm requirements before booking. Even with a skipper, many boats are available to hire without formal qualifications.

High season runs from June to September, when the Levante wind can build afternoon chop. For calmest seas, book a morning slot between 9am and 1pm.

At a glance

  • Price band: €50, €5,000 per person/tour
  • Peak months: June, September 2026
  • Trip length: 3 hours to full-day
  • Capacity: 6, 12 guests typical
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I rent a boat in Marbella without a skipper?

Yes, but you must hold a valid boating licence recognised in Spain, and the operator will confirm the required certification.

What is the best time of year for a boat trip in Marbella?

The best conditions are May, June, September, and October, with calm seas and fewer crowds than July and August.

How many people can a boat rental in Marbella accommodate?

Most rentals cater for six to twelve guests, but larger yachts are available for up to twenty people.

Are there any hidden fees when booking a boat in Marbella?

The listing price usually includes the boat and fuel for a standard itinerary, but extras like crew tips, food, and beverages are separate.

To explore your options further, you can compare verified boat listings and find the right vessel for your day on the water. Visit BoatHire24 to book or list your boat in Marbella for 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
